volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Two Bacchises (Section 1.0.1 - Section 1.1.74b)

sections.label: Section 1.0.1
sections.original:
1
quíbus ingenium in animo utibilest , modicum et sine vernilitate
sections.translation: ⋯ those who are [*] of a thrifty turn of mind , modest , and without servility . ⋯ ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.2
sections.original:
2
víncla , virgaé , molae : saévitudo mala fit peior
sections.translation: Chains , rods , and mills ; their shocking brutality becomes worse . ⋯ ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.3
sections.translation: Sweep out the house [*] with brooms , work briskly .

sections.label: Section 1.0.4
sections.translation: ⋯ Will some one call

sections.label: Section 1.0.5
sections.original:
4
cum nassiterna et cum aqua istum impurissimum ?
sections.translation: that most dirty fellow with the water-pail [*] and the water .

sections.label: Section 1.0.6
sections.translation: As like as milk [*] is to milk ;

sections.label: Section 1.0.7
sections.translation: ⋯ She was of the same name with myself . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.8
sections.original:
7
( latro ) suam qui auro vitam venditat
sections.translation: The soldier who sells his life for gold ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.9
sections.original:
8
sció spiritum éius maiórem esse multo
sections.translation: I know that his breath is much stronger than when the bellows of bull ’ s hide are blowing , when the rocks melt where the iron is made .

sections.label: Section 1.0.11b
sections.translation: Of what country did he seem to you ?

sections.label: Section 1.0.12
sections.original:
8
Praenéstinum opino esse , ita erat gloriosus .
sections.translation: I think he is of Praeneste [*] ; he was such a boaster .

sections.label: Section 1.0.13
sections.original:
9
néque id haud subditiva glória oppidum arbitror .
sections.translation: ⋯ The city ⋯ and I don ’ t think it is in spurious boastfulness . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.14
sections.original:
10
ne a quóquam acciperes alio mercedem annuam ,
sections.translation: that from no one else you might be receiving a yearly pay , except from oneself , nor be toying with any man ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.16
sections.translation: ⋯ like slugs upon a man .

sections.label: Section 1.0.17
sections.translation: ⋯ My heart , my hope [*] ,

sections.label: Section 1.0.18
sections.translation: my honey , my sweetness , my nutriment , my delight . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.19
sections.translation: Let me bestow on you my love ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.20
sections.translation: Has Cupid or has love overpowered you ? ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.21
sections.translation: I have heard [*] that Ulysses underwent toils innumerable , who , in wanderings , was twenty years away from his native land . But this young man by far outdoes Ulysses ; who here in this spot is wandering within the walls of the city . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.25
sections.translation: whatever is her name ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.26
sections.original:
17
quaé sodalem átque me exércitos habet
sections.translation: She who keeps my friend and me engaged . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.27
sections.original:
18
nam crédo cuivis excantare cor potes .
sections.translation: ⋯ for I really do believe that with ease you can enchant the heart of any man [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.0.28
sections.original:
19
sin lénocinium forte collibitum est tibi ,
sections.translation: ⋯ But if a life [*] of wantonness is perchance preferred by you , consider the price that I agreed to give you that at that age you might not be following me for nought ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.0.31
sections.translation: the Arabian ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.1.35
sections.original: Quíd si hoc potis est út tu taceas , égo loquar ?
sections.translation: And suppose it is much better that you should hold your peace , and I should speak ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.35b
sections.translation: With pleasure ; you may proceed .

sections.label: Section 1.1.36
sections.original: Vbi me fugiet memoria , ibi tu facito ut subvenias , soror .
sections.translation: When my memory shall fail me , then do you take care to aid me , sister .

sections.label: Section 1.1.37
sections.original: Pol magis metuo , ne defuerit mi in monendo oratio .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m more afraid that I shan ’ t have the choice of prompting you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.38
sections.original: Pol ego metuo , lusciniolae ne defuerit cantio .
sections.translation: Troth , I ’ m afraid the little nightingale may lose her powers [*] of song . Follow this way .

sections.label: Section 1.1.39b
sections.original: Quid agunt duae germanae meretrices cognomines ?
sections.translation: What are these two Courtesans , the namesake-sisters , about ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.40
sections.translation: What have you been planning in your consultation ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.40b
sections.translation: What ’ s proper ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.40c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , that doesn ’ t belong to the Courtesan .

sections.label: Section 1.1.41
sections.translation: Nothing is there more wretched than a woman .

sections.label: Section 1.1.41b
sections.translation: What , say you , is there more worthy of it ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.42
sections.original: Haec ita me orat , sibi qui caveat aliquem ut hominem reperiam ,
sections.translation: This sister of mine entreats me so to find some person to protect her against this Captain ; that when she as served her time [*] he may bring her back home . Do you , I entreat you , be her protector .

sections.label: Section 1.1.44b
sections.translation: Why should I protect her ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.44c
sections.translation: That she may be brought home again ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.45
sections.original: ubi ei dediderit operas , ne hanc ille habeat pro ancilla sibi ;
sections.translation: when she has fulfilled her engagement to him , so that he may not take her as a servant [*] for himself . But , if she had the gold to pay him back again , gladly would she do so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.47
sections.translation: Where now is this person ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.47b
sections.original: Iam hic credo aderit . sed hoc idem apud nos rectius
sections.translation: I expect that he ’ ll be here just now . But this you ’ ll be able to arrange better among ourselves ; and sitting there , you shall wait until he comes . So you will drink some wine , and so , I ’ ll give you a kiss when you have drunk it .

sections.label: Section 1.1.50
sections.translation: Your coaxing is mere birdlime .

sections.label: Section 1.1.50b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.50c
sections.translation: Because , in fact , I understand how you two are aiming at one poor pigeon , myself ; ( aside ) very nearly is the limed reed [*] breaking my feathers . Madam , I judge that such deeds befit me not .

sections.label: Section 1.1.53
sections.translation: Why so , I pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.53b
sections.original: Quia , Bacchis , bacchas metuo et bacchanal tuom .
sections.translation: Bacchis , it is , because I dread you Bacchantes , and your Bacchanalian den [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.54
sections.original: Quid est ? quid metuis ? ne tibi lectus malitiam apud me suadeat ?
sections.translation: What is it that you dread ? Surely , not that the couch in my house may lead you into mischief ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.55
sections.original: Magis illectum tuom quam lectum metuo . mala tu es bestia .
sections.translation: Your allurements [*] , more than your couch , do I dread . You ’ re a mischievous serpent [*] . But , madam , a lurking-place does not befit this youthful age .

sections.label: Section 1.1.57
sections.original: Egomet , apud me si quid stulte facere cupias , prohibeam .
sections.translation: Should you wish at my house to do anything that ’ s unwise , I myself should hinder it . But , when the Captain comes , I wish you to be at my house for this reason ; because , when you are present , no one will do her and me any injury .

sections.label: Section 1.1.60
sections.original: tu prohibebis , et eadem opera tuo sodali operam dabis ;
sections.translation: You will prevent that , and by the same means you will be aiding your friend ; this Captain , too , on arriving , will suspect that I am your mistress . Why are you silent , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.62b
sections.translation: Because these things are pleasant in the talking of ; but in the practice , and when you make trial , the same are armed with stings . They pierce the feelings , goad one ’ s fortune , and wound one ’ s merits and character .

sections.label: Section 1.1.65
sections.translation: What do you dread from her ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.65b
sections.original: Quid ego metuam rogitas ? adulescens homo
sections.translation: What do I dread , do you ask ? Am I a person in my youth to enter a place of exercise [*] of such a nature , where people sweat to their undoing ?— where for the quoit I rereive a loss , disgrace , too , for my running ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.68
sections.translation: How charmingly you do talk .

sections.label: Section 1.1.68b
sections.original: Vbi ego capiam pro machaera turturem ,
sections.translation: Where I ’ m to take a turtle-dove [*] instead of a sword , and where another puts into my hand the goblet [*] instead of the cestus ; the drinking-cup [*] is in place of the helmet , the wreathed garland instead of the crest [*] ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.71
sections.original: pro hasta talos , pro lorica malacum capiam pallium ,
sections.translation: the dice in place of the lance . For the coat of mail I should have to assume a soft cloak [*] ; where , too , in place of a horse a couch must be given me ,— for shield , a strumpet [*] may be lying by me . Avaunt from me — avaunt !

sections.label: Section 1.1.73b
sections.translation: O , you ’ re too fierce .

sections.label: Section 1.1.73c
sections.translation: I am attending to my own interests .

sections.label: Section 1.1.73d
sections.translation: You must be softened down ; and , in fact , I offer you my aid in this .

sections.label: Section 1.1.74b
sections.translation: But you are too expensive an assistant .
